extraction.one_line_summary: GAVIRIA Fernando took a wind-affected sprint after Quick-Step split the field with 3-8km to go using coastal crosswinds.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step exploited exposed coastal crosswinds between 8-5km to go, creating decisive time gaps before sheltered headwind finish.
HAAS Nathan attacked with 400m in headwind conditions, forcing RICHEZE Maximiliano to close and disrupting rival sprint trains.
Teams chose shallow-section wheels yet still struggled with bike control, suggesting wind strength exceeded forecast expectations.

rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: won after Quick-Step's echelon split the field, showing combination of team tactics and sprint strength
rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: positive: aggressive late attack into headwind demonstrated willingness to gamble rather than accept pack sprint defeat
rider_signals.RICHEZE Maximiliano: positive: shut down HAAS attack inside 400m, showing leadout riders can neutralize last-minute surprises in headwinds
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: neutral: positioned well through exposed section but lacked Quick-Step's echelon support to challenge for win
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: neutral: captured intermediate sprint for Quick-Step, suggesting team already controlled race pace early
